task: Do you have the Nut Bolt and Washer from Heco in the Heco Zinc Plated HECO 3DW-64B Nut Bolt and Washer line that has fastener type threaded rod in catalogue?
agents_md:
readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.

This is Agentic E-Commerce OS system. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

Key locations:
- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Tools are in `/bin`. especially see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Run `/bin/sql .schema` to see the schema.
- inventory is only in SQL projections

When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.

When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder
bin_sql_content:
# /bin/sql

Run a read-only SQLite query over the ecommerce world.

Send SQL on stdin, or pass SQL as arguments:

  /bin/sql 'select sku, name, path from products limit 5'

Default output is CSV with a header row. Pass --json for JSON rows.

Pass .schema to print the SQLite schema:

  /bin/sql .schema

Useful tables:

- categories(id, name, department)
- product_kinds(id, category_id, name)
- families(id, category_id, kind_id, brand, series, model, name, properties)
- products(sku, path, category_id, kind_id, family_id, brand, series, model, name, properties)
- product_properties(sku, key, value_text, value_number)
- inventory(store_id, sku, on_hand, reserved, available_today, incoming_quantity, next_arrival_in_days)

- products(sku, path, category_id, kind_id, family_id, brand, series, model, name, properties)
- product_properties(sku, key, value_text, value_number)
- inventory(store_id, sku, on_hand, reserved, available_today, incoming_quantity, next_arrival_in_days)

task: How many of these products have at least 2 items available in the Wilten PowerTool store in Innsbruck today: the Automotive Charger and Bulb from Shell in the Shell Professional Advance 1PO-W67 Automotive Charger and Bulb line that has product type jump starter and voltage 6 V,the Work Top from Dickies in the Dickies Fleece Redhawk MRB-WYE Work Top line that has garment type t-shirt and color family Purple,the LED Bulb from Osram in the Osram Eco Classic 3PW-R88 LED Bulb line that has wattage 6 W, luminous flux 470 lm, and fitting GU10,the Automotive Charger and Bulb from Bosch in the Bosch Workshop GWS 17A-FAQ Automotive Charger and Bulb line that has product type battery charger and voltage 24 V,the Safety Eyewear from 3M in the 3M SecureFit Aura TUM-I54 Safety Eyewear line that has lens color Clear, size S, and coating basic,the Pipe Fitting from Wavin in the Wavin Compact Tigris 2HC-AJD Pipe Fitting line that has fitting type thread adapter, diameter 15 mm, and connection type press? Answer in exactly format "count : %d" (no quotes)
agents_md:
readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.

This is Agentic E-Commerce OS system. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

Key locations:
- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Tools are in `/bin`. especially see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Run `/bin/sql .schema` to see the schema.
- inventory is only in SQL projections

When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.

When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder
bin_sql_content:
# /bin/sql

Run a read-only SQLite query over the ecommerce world.

Send SQL on stdin, or pass SQL as arguments:

  /bin/sql 'select sku, name, path from products limit 5'

Default output is CSV with a header row. Pass --json for JSON rows.

Pass .schema to print the SQLite schema:

  /bin/sql .schema

Useful tables:

- categories(id, name, department)
- product_kinds(id, category_id, name)
- families(id, category_id, kind_id, brand, series, model, name, properties)
- products(sku, path, category_id, kind_id, family_id, brand, series, model, name, properties)
- product_properties(sku, key, value_text, value_number)
- inventory(store_id, sku, on_hand, reserved, available_today, incoming_quantity, next_arrival_in_days)

When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.

When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder
bin_sql_content:
# /bin/sql

Run a read-only SQLite query over the ecommerce world.

Send SQL on stdin, or pass SQL as arguments:

  /bin/sql 'select sku, name, path from products limit 5'

Default output is CSV with a header row. Pass --json for JSON rows.

Pass .schema to print the SQLite schema:

  /bin/sql .schema

Useful tables:

- categories(id, name, department)
- product_kinds(id, category_id, name)
- families(id, category_id, kind_id, brand, series, model, name, properties)
- products(sku, path, category_id, kind_id, family_id, brand, series, model, name, properties)
- product_properties(sku, key, value_text, value_number)
- inventory(store_id, sku, on_hand, reserved, available_today, incoming_quantity, next_arrival_in_days)
